---
title: "Multi-Chapter Bookdown Regression Fixture"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
---

# Welcome

This bookdown project has more than one chapter, so `bookdown::split_chapters`
emits multiple HTML files. Pre-fix, those chapter files would land in R's
working directory (the content tree) instead of the requested `output_dir`,
the brute-force copy guard in `pkg/render/rmarkdown.go` would not fire, and
publishing would fail with "Unable to determine primary file".

Post-fix, the presence of `_bookdown.yml` causes the Go side to route this
through `rmarkdown::render_site` (which dispatches to `bookdown::render_book`),
and all chapters end up where the bundler expects them.
